articles.title: Ex-Google CEO Eric Schmidt booed after AI remarks at Arizona commencement
articles.description: Pew research shows Americans are more worried than excited about AI as graduates voiced fears over jobs A former Google CEO, Eric Schmidt, was met with students’ boos at a university commencement address in Arizona on Sunday when he raised the topic of artificial intelligence (AI) and its effects. Schmidt – who led the tech giant for more than a decade, acquiring a multi-billion dollar fortune in the process – was speaking to as many as 10,000 graduating University of Arizona students when he addressed the impact of modern technology on society. Continue reading...

articles.title: Trump cuts to weather data could make forecasts less reliable, warn experts
articles.description: Use of AI is a valuable tool for weather prediction but only when it’s trained with ample data, experts say As the US prepares for hurricane season and a summer of record-breaking heat, experts fear the Trump administration’s cuts to climate and weather data programming could make the federal government’s weather forecasts less reliable when they are needed most. The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (Noaa) late last year launched a suite of artificial intelligence-powered global weather forecast models which it said would improve “speed, efficiency, and accuracy”. In March, an agency official said those models are being trained with centuries of weather data. Continue reading...

articles.title: AI in radio: Why ChatGPT, Gemini and Grok still struggle to replace human hosts
articles.description: An unusual experiment by San Francisco-based startup Andon Labs asked leading AI models to run fully autonomous radio stations for five months. The outcome was less a glimpse of flawless machine creativity and more an unpredictable mix of emotional breakdowns, awkward humour, strange ethical dilemmas, and surprisingly human-sounding mistakes.

articles.title: B by Lenskart smart glasses launched for early access: Price, features, and all the details
articles.description: Lenskart has launched B by Lenskart, its first pair of AI smart glasses, on early access in India at Rs 22,000. The commercial price is set at Rs 27,000. The 40-gram frame packs a 12 MP Sony camera, Qualcomm's Snapdragon AR1 chip, and a Gemini-powered assistant called Buddy that handles over 40 languages, including Hinglish, and sees what the wearer is looking at.

Benioff emphasised that AI tools enhance engineer productivity and efficiency, rather than replacing them, as Salesforce continues to invest heavily in AI across its operations.

articles.description: Sony's new INZONE H6 Air gaming headset has arrived in India, boasting an ultra-light 199g design and an open-back structure for natural, 3D sound. Inspired by professional studio monitors, it features a unique graduated hole design and supports 360 Spatial Sound for Gaming. Priced at Rs 17,990, it offers versatile connectivity and a detachable mic.

articles.title: Genmoji In iOS 27 Will Study Your Chats And Photos: Here's What It Does With Them
articles.description:
Apple is working on a new twist to its Genmoji feature that could make it far more personal. According to a report, the company plans to introduce suggested Genmoji in iOS 27, with the system drawing from your photo library and keyboard history to generate emoji ideas automatically. The feature is optional, meaning users who are uncomfortable with it can simply turn it off.

It is a small but telling move from Apple, showing the company is still trying to get more people to actually use Genmoji after a lukewarm reception since its debut.

How Genmoji Has Evolved Since Its Launch

Genmoji first arrived with iOS 18.2 as part of Apple's initial Apple Intelligence rollout. The idea was straightforward: users type a prompt, and the system uses image generation models to create a custom emoji in a style consistent with Apple's existing emoji design. Results were mixed, with the feature producing some solid outputs but also some noticeable misses.

ALSO READ: Buying iPhone 17 Pro? Skip Amazon And Flipkart; These Stores Are Cheaper

Apple refined it in iOS 26, adding deeper customisation options and the ability to blend two emojis together. Now, according to Mark Gurman's Power On newsletter, iOS 27 will take things further with Suggested Genmoji.

A new toggle found in the keyboard settings of iOS 27 and iPadOS 27 reads: "Suggested Genmoji are created from your photos and your commonly typed phrases." The feature will be optional when the update rolls out.

What The New Suggested Genmoji Feature Actually Means

If the implementation is done well, the feature could surface genuinely relevant and personalised emoji suggestions rather than generic ones. The potential is there, but so is a reasonable concern: not everyone will be comfortable with a system that automatically generates content based on their photos and typing patterns.

ALSO READ: Google Built Pixel 10 To Convince iPhone Users To Make The Jump

Apple's decision to make it opt-in should ease those concerns for most users. It also remains unclear whether Genmoji will continue relying entirely on on-device models in iOS 27, though the absence of any reported image model upgrade suggests that may still be the case.

articles.title: Microsoft is retiring Teams’ Together Mode in favour of ...
articles.description: Microsoft is retiring its Together Mode feature in Teams on June 30, opting for a streamlined meeting experience centered on the Gallery view. This consolidation aims to simplify interfaces, reduce complexity, and enhance video quality and performance for all users. The move promises fewer clicks and smoother video, especially on less powerful devices, allowing for faster innovation in core features.

articles.title: Salesforce rewrites roles amid AI shift
articles.description: Salesforce is undergoing an AI-led transformation, redesigning jobs and reskilling its entire workforce to integrate AI agents into daily operations. Traditional roles are evolving into outcome-based positions, with AI handling repetitive tasks and employees focusing on higher-value work. The company emphasizes AI fluency, making it a core requirement for both existing employees and new hires.

articles.title: The Unfiltered Snabbit Story | The Startup Solving India’s Unorganised House Help Crisis
articles.description: AI may be transforming the world — but some of the biggest everyday problems still need human solutions. In this episode of The Business Today Podcast, Sakshi Batra sits down with Aayush Agarwal, Founder & CEO of Snabbit, the hyperlocal home services startup that scaled to over 50,000 jobs a day in less than a year. At a time when startups are racing toward AI-first models, Snabbit is building something very different: a human-first company solving one of urban India’s oldest and most frustrating problems — reliable house help. From managing 15,000+ service professionals across 140+ micro-markets to building trust, consistency, and operational discipline at scale, Aayush shares the unfiltered reality of building a category-defining startup in one of India’s toughest markets.

This trend aligns with the central government's clean energy initiatives and a call to reduce fossil fuel consumption.

articles.title: Jim Bianco, head of one of American research company, agrees that AI panic is everywhere, but what AI doom-and-gloom story is missing
articles.description: Jim Bianco, a macro strategist, urges optimism regarding AI, arguing that fears of widespread job losses are misplaced. He explains AI's potential to streamline complex software stacks, freeing employees for higher-value tasks like judgment and strategy. Citing economic history, Bianco suggests AI could boost demand for skilled workers, similar to the internet's impact post-dot-com bubble.

articles.title: All Biomods in Subnautica 2 and how to get them
articles.description: Subnautica 2 introduces Biomods, genetic modifications unlocked by scanning alien creatures, offering unique Active and Passive abilities. These range from enhanced movement and resource detection to predator distraction and improved survival stats like water and food efficiency. Players can also gain defensive and utility benefits such as threat alerts and bioluminescence.

articles.title: Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi on AI, EVs & Why India Could Become Uber’s Biggest Market
articles.description: Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi joins ETtech.com editor Samidha Sharma and Pranav Mukul for a wide-ranging conversation on India’s exploding mobility market, AI-powered engineering, EV adoption, bike taxis, autonomous vehicles, and why Uber believes India could eventually become its largest global market. From partnerships with the Adani Group to the future of food delivery, logistics, robotaxis, and AI agents booking rides. Dara explains how Uber is repositioning itself as a next-generation mobility and logistics platform. The conversation also dives into India’s startup ecosystem, public transport integration, social security for gig workers, and whether AI will augment or replace jobs.
